title: Projections, Eigenvectors, and SVD
learningObjectives: Connect data variance to projection through SVD

whyThisLesson.text: BIG lesson. Three connected concepts. These form the linear algebraic foundation for dimensionality reduction and understanding representation geometry.

intuition.analogy: Projection is like casting a shadow. Eigenvectors are directions that don't get knocked off course by a transformation. SVD is separating a transformation into rotation, stretching, and another rotation.
intuition.analogyLimit: High dimensional spaces have many orthogonal directions that are hard to visualize.
intuition.text: SVD connects data to variance to projection.

steps.insight: Splits a vector into parallel and perpendicular components.

visual.question: What happens to a circle under a linear transformation?
visual.description: A circle stretches into an ellipse along its eigenvectors.

technical.text: SVD: X=UΣV^T. FVU: ||X-X̂||²/||X||² measures the fraction of variance unexplained by a low-rank approximation.

mathematics.label: Projection
mathematics.plainExplanation: Vector v cast onto direction u.
mathematics.whyNeeded: To find component of v along u.

mathematics.label: Eigenvector
mathematics.plainExplanation: Vector whose direction is unchanged by C.
mathematics.whyNeeded: To find principal axes of transformation.

mathematics.label: Fraction of Variance Unexplained (FVU)
mathematics.plainExplanation: Ratio of squared error to total squared norm.

evidence.label: [Eckart-Young Theorem]
evidence.paraphrase: Truncated SVD provides the best low-rank approximation in terms of Frobenius norm.

failureModes.name: Equating eigenvectors with concepts
failureModes.mechanism: Assuming directions of max variance are independent concepts.
failureModes.diagnosticTest: Check if features are orthogonal in reality.

setconcaConnection.proposedExperiment: Compare SVD components vs SetConCA features.

exercises.prompt: What is the projection of [3,4] onto [1,0]?

masteryQuestions.prompt: What does an eigenvalue represent for a covariance matrix?
masteryQuestions.goodVersion: It represents the amount of variance in the dataset along the corresponding eigenvector direction.

bridgeToNext.text: Now we apply these concepts to PCA.
